'''Matt Whitley''' (born 20 January 1996) is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[England Knights]] at international level.

He previously played for the [[Widnes Vikings]] and [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Whitley was born in [[Billinge, Merseyside|Billinge]], [[Merseyside, England]].

==Career==
===Widnes Vikings===
He made his début for Widnes on 10 April 2015 against the [[Warrington Wolves]], and signed a full-time contract with the club two weeks later.<ref>{{cite web|title=Widnes Vikings: Teenage star Matt Whitley signs new three-year deal|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/32432885|website=BBC Sport|access-date=16 May 2015|date=23 April 2015}}</ref>

===Catalans Dragons===
On 9 October 2021, Whitley played for [[Catalans Dragons]] in their [[2021 Super League Grand Final]] defeat against St. Helens.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10-12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
On 14 October 2023, Whitley played in Catalans [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===St Helens===
On 18 October 2023, Whitley signed a two-year deal to join his boyhood club [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=18 Oct 2023|title=St Helens make third signing for 2024 in shape of Grand Finalist Matt Whitley from Catalans Dragons|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/st-helens-make-third-signing-for-2024-in-shape-of-grand-finalist-matt-whitley-from-catalans-dragons}}</ref>
In round 1 of the 2024 Super League season, Whitley made his club debut for St Helens and scored two tries as the club defeated the newly promoted [[London Broncos]] 40-4.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68304742|title=Super League: St Helens 40-4 London Broncos - Saints thrash promoted side|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Whitley played 20 matches for St Helens in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish sixth on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2024/sep/28/warrington-st-helens-super-league-eliminator-playoff-rugby-league-match-report/|title=
‘Soul destroying’: Warrington eliminate St Helens in golden drop goal thriller|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>
Whitley played 30 games for St Helens in the 2025 Super League season including the clubs 20-12 semi-final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c04q6rqyrv6o/|title=Retiring Waerea-Hargreaves set to miss Grand Final|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

==International career==
In July 2018 he was selected in the [[England Knights]] Performance squad.<ref name="Ten new additions in England Knights squad">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ten-new-additions-in-england-knights-squad/|title=Ten new additions in England Knights squad|publisher=loverugbyleague|date=24 July 2018|accessdate=31 October 2019}}</ref> Later that year he was selected for the England Knights on their tour of [[Papua New Guinea]]. He played against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] at the Oil Search National Football Stadium.<ref name="Papua New Guinea vs England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718007#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea vs England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=3 November 2018|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref>
